learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate culturally safe communication and interpersonal skills with clients, family members and other professionals and peers (SPA PS 1.1-1.7)
Apply clinical reasoning to select and administer appropriate assessments and analyse and interpret data to identify client communication and swallowing needs (SPA PS 3.2, 3.3)
Design and implement evidence-based, person- and family-centred management plans as required to support client communication and swallowing needs (SPA PS 1.1-1.7, 2.5, 3.4, 3.5)
Critically reflect on your professional abilities to set goals and professional development plans in the context of life-long learning (SPA PS 2.1, 2.2, 2.3)
Produce written and verbal clinical records which are appropriate to the clinical setting and the audience (SPA PS 1.2, 1.3, 1.5, 3.3)
Engage in interprofessional collaborative practice to achieve respectful partnerships across professions (SPA PS 1.4, 1.5, 1.7, 2.5, 2.6)

assessments.due_date: Your COMPASS®3.0 assessment will take place at the midway point and final placement week.
assessments.task_description:
Students will be assessed by their practice educator at the midway point and at the end of their placement using COMPASS®3.0 Online. Students are required to complete a self-evaluation prior to their meeting with their practice educator.

Under no circumstances should identifiable clinical information be uploaded to open or external AI platforms, as it is unethical and irresponsible. Given this assessment is related to a placement with clinical information please see more information on use of AI in student placements under additional assessment information.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Practice Educators should submit their final evaluation of students using COMPASS®3.0 online no later than one (1) week following the completion of the placement.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

Reflection Part 1 is due the day before the scheduled tutorial in Weeks 2-6.

Reflection Part 2 is due at the end of the scheduled tutorial in Weeks 2-6.
assessments.task_description:
You will attend tutorials to support your preparation for placement. To develop your reflective practice skills, you will complete reflection tasks in Weeks 2-6.

Reflection Part 1 must be submitted via the SPCH3213 Blackboard site before each tutorial in Weeks 2-6. You will respond to prompts such as:

  1. What I think I know - identify two (2) things you know or have experienced on placement about this topic
  2. What I want to find out - identify two (2) things you would like to find out from the tutorial.


Reflection Part 2 must be submitted by the end of the tutorial, following discussion of your reflections with a peer. You will respond to a prompt such as:

  1. What I know now - what you have learnt from the tutorial


Across the 5 weeks of tutorials, you are required to submit a total of 3 reflections (both part A and B). Each reflection is PASS/FAIL MUST PASS and must be passed to pass the course overall.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Placement

Students will be placed in a sessional speech pathology (internal or external) clinic with a practice educator. Students will be placed in a sessional adult or paediatric placement with a practice educator. Placement duration will be either:

• 1 full day per week for 8 weeks of the semester or;

• half a day per week for 13 weeks of the semester (Weeks 1-13).

Start/finish times for placements will vary depending on the placement allocated to each student.
learning_activities.topic_description: Placement Students will be placed in a sessional speech pathology (internal or external) clinic with a practice educator. Students will be placed in a sessional adult or paediatric placement with a practice educator. Placement duration will be either: • 1 full day per week for 8 weeks of the semester or; • half a day per week for 13 weeks of the semester (Weeks 1-13). Start/finish times for placements will vary depending on the placement allocated to each student.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorials

Students will attend tutorials (2-hours) in Weeks 1-8 to support preparation for placement. Tutorials will cover a range of professional practice topics.
